Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Visual overhaul: dramatically raise text contrast across site

Previously most text used near-black-on-near-black colors (#3a3020,
#2a2010) that were almost invisible. Riff on veritable-games clear
contrast hierarchy, applied in warm amber tones.

- Nav links: #3a3020 → #6a5a40; hover now jumps directly to amber
- Global body: slight raise to #c0b088
- Homepage body text: #9a8a68 → #b8a880; blockquote: #5a4e34 → #7a6a4a
- Book titles: #8a7a58 → #c0b088 (primary content, biggest win)
- Author/year meta: #3a3020 → #6a5a40
- Cat-labels, count, placeholders: #2a2010 → #4e4232–#5a4e38
- Suggestion pills: #4a3e26 → #7a6a48
- Context menu links: #6a5c3a → #8a7858
- Raised border opacities throughout for cleaner separation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): stop virtualizer.measure() from destroying cached measurements (#295)

Root cause: virtualizer.measure() clears the entire item-size cache,
forcing all items back to estimateSize() values. Because React ref
callbacks don't re-fire on re-render (same element, same ref identity),
measureElement never re-measures existing items, leaving them with
wildly inaccurate estimated sizes — producing 30,000+ pixel gaps.

Changes:
- Remove the triple-measure useEffect that called virtualizer.measure()
  on every message change (immediate + 150ms + 500ms delays)
- Replace container-level ResizeObserver (which also called measure())
  with per-item ResizeObservers that call measureElement on each node
- Cap estimateSize at 400px so even if estimates are wrong, gaps are
  bounded to a reasonable maximum before measurement corrects them

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: IGST incorrectly applied for customers with GSTIN but gst_category=Unregistered

Root cause: get_place_of_supply() used the branch's custom_place_of_supply for
ANY Unregistered customer (even those with a GSTIN), while is_inter_state_supply()
correctly guarded with "and not customer_gstin". This divergence meant the POS
tax template used GSTIN state (→ IGST), but the invoice's place_of_supply was
set to branch state (→ Punjab/03), causing India Compliance to reject with
"Cannot charge IGST for intra-state supplies".

Fixes:
- gst_tax.py: add `and not customer_gstin` guard to get_place_of_supply(),
  making it consistent with is_inter_state_supply()
- CreateCustomerDialog.vue: in createGstCustomer, treat gst_category="Unregistered"
  as invalid when a GSTIN is present — default to "Registered Regular" so newly
  created GST customers are never saved as Unregistered

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: dramatically speed up Convert and Compress

Convert to MP4:
- Switch from full re-encode (libx264 medium) to stream copy (-c:v copy)
- Only re-encodes audio to AAC for compatibility
- MKV/MOV/AVI → MP4 is now near-instant (remux only, no video decode/encode)

Compress presets:
- whatsapp/instagram/tiktok: fast → veryfast (3x faster, imperceptible quality diff at CRF 26)
- max-quality: medium → fast (2x faster, minimal quality diff at CRF 18)

- Updated header/footer across all 321 pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: ship codex/ship-20260302-014244 to main (#249)

* feat: canonical file discovery module unifying all project scope boundaries

Replace 10+ independent file discovery functions with a single canonical
module (`lintgate/discovery.py`) backed by `git ls-files` with `os.walk`
fallback. Fixes mutants/ being linted as project code, the decomposition.py
os.walk bug (dirnames not pruned in-place), and inconsistent exclusion sets
across tools. Addresses #245, #246, #247, #248.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: harden mutation pipeline — source protection, path validation, scope hygiene

Fix stale pyproject.toml paths_to_mutate (was absolute pytest temp path),
add backup/restore mechanism to survive process kills during mutmut runs,
guard all mutation entry points against mutants/ directory paths, and add
belt-and-suspenders exclusions to .gitignore and .qlty/qlty.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove 22 redundant test files, skip benchmarks in CI

Delete auto-generated coverage gap fillers (batch1-5), numbered bugfix
pass files (pass1-3), _cov gap supplements never merged back into main
test files (4 files), redundant zz_manual smoke/scratch tests (9 files),
and a duplicate phase2 file. Mark benchmarks to skip by default.

Reduces test suite from 5,954 to 5,681 tests. Full suite now runs in
~84s with xdist parallelism vs 10+ minutes previously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: raise xdist worker floor to 2 in pre-push hook

On loaded machines (load > cores), the old formula yielded workers=1,
causing the full test suite to run single-threaded with coverage — taking
10+ minutes. Floor of 2 ensures parallelism even under load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: wrap tests.yml matrix expression with fromJSON()

GitHub Actions requires fromJSON() to parse a dynamic string
expression into an array for matrix strategy. Without it, the
workflow fails at YAML parse time with "workflow file issue",
blocking Tests (3.11) and Tests (3.12) required checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fix mutation engine src/ layout mismatch and decompose high-complexity modules

Add _path_to_mutmut_module() replicating mutmut v3's naming (strip src.
prefix, collapse .__init__) so mutant IDs match actual mutmut output for
src-layout projects. Thread project_root through prescribe pipeline to
fix absolute-path template imports. Extract mutant_parsing.py from
engine.py, decompose mutation_channel.execute into focused helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update test_phase4_function_filter import after mutant_parsing extraction

extract_func_name_from_mutant_id was moved from engine.py to
mutant_parsing.py during the decomposition refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: auto-cleanup stale disposable branches (dependabot, codex/ship)

The schedule handler only cleaned merged branches, so unmerged
dependabot and ship branches accumulated forever. Now disposable-
prefixed branches (dependabot/*, codex/ship-*) are closed and
deleted after 7 days if still unmerged, with a comment on any
open PR before closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(create-vertz-app): add bunfig.toml and bun-plugin-shim to scaffold (#903)

* fix(create-vertz-app): add bunfig.toml and bun-plugin-shim to scaffold

Scaffolded apps were missing the bunfig.toml and bun-plugin-shim.ts files
that register the Vertz compiler plugin for Bun's dev server client bundler.
Without these, client-side JSX was compiled by Bun's native JSX runtime
(jsxDEV/document.createElement) instead of the Vertz compiler (__element),
causing hydration to silently fail — SSR content vanished after client JS loaded.

Also adds @vertz/ui-server to devDependencies since bun-plugin-shim imports from it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add changeset for create-vertz-app scaffold fix
